subject: Electrotherapy II
definition: Biofeedback is a treatment technique in which people are trained to improve their health by using signals from their own bodies, usually via electromyographic (EMG) monitoring.
clinicalPearls: Focus on initial simple motor tasks to build patient confidence before advancing to complex functional activities.
vivaQuestions: What is the target parameter measured in surface EMG biofeedback?

mcqs.question: Biofeedback primarily acts as a tool for:
mcqs.explanation: EMG Biofeedback senses muscle electrical activity to help patients learn conscious monitoring, re-education, or relaxation of specific muscle groups.
